old_claim.note: Artillery & Fortification Schools provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Depot served a military-technical milieu in which engineering and artillery required geometry, ballistics, and siegecraft, making fortification and artillery schooling contextual rather than a strict enabler.

source_shape.source_locator: Arabesques article says many Depot printed and manuscript works corresponded to the rise of engineering and artillery, requiring geometry, ballistics, and siegecraft.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source links the Depot collection to engineering and artillery knowledge rather than saying schools caused the Depot.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This justifies weakening the edge to a shared military-technical context: fortification and artillery schooling belong in the same knowledge ecosystem but are not shown as a direct enabling mechanism.

ontology_before: Military Map Rooms treated Artillery & Fortification Schools as a generic enabling prerequisite.
ontology_after: Depot de la Guerre Military Map Archive keeps fortification and artillery schooling only as a contextual common dependency for the same military-technical field.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: artillery_fortification_schools remains a direct edge but is retyped from enabling to common_dependency.
would_reject_if: A source showed the 1688 Depot was founded as an output of artillery or fortification schools.
The artillery_fortification_schools node were narrowed to the exact instructional archive inside the Depot.
The Depot node were split and a later military engineering curriculum node took this edge instead.
short_reason: The source supports shared military-technical context, not a direct enabling prerequisite.
